title: Strange Visual Errors in 222
wikitext:
[]: Strange Visual Errors in 2.22 (Spoilers, Lots of Images)             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 06, 2010 7:59 pm The thing about making a film that encourages people to go through frame-by-frame with secret nipples and creepy faces is that people will end up noticing countless little animation errors. Here are some of the weirder ones that I've found. They shouldn't be attributed to laziness, considering over a thousand shots were changed between 2.0 and 2.22 , but I guess just to oversight. First, when Unit 05 stabs the Third Angel's entry plug, or dummy plug, or whatever kind of plug it is, the plug disappears... but the plug's shadow remains. The plug is visible again in the next shot: Later on, at the aquarium, the fish are always swimming along in the background. Always, that is, except in this one shot (cropped to emphasize fish): Strangely, the only shot in which the fish are completely motionless is the longest held shot at the picnic in which the fish are visible. When Asuka is cooking a meal for Shinji Hikari, a sign is visible with text that doesn't quite know where to stop. Something's horribly wrong with these birds... can you tell what it is? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:21 pm A small contribution from me: I found two scenes where the desks reflex are considerably off than the rest. [URL=http://img96.imageshack.us/i/grab60090.jpg/] [/URL] [URL=http://img690.imageshack.us/i/grab63144.jpg/] [/URL] also i know it isnpt the RoE but i remembered this: anyone ever saw this before?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:25 pm What looks wrong about the reflections? ...Or the EoE shot?

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:31 pm NAveryW wrote: What looks wrong about the reflections? ...Or the EoE shot?

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:32 pm NAveryW wrote: ...Or the EoE shot?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:43 pm NatTheWriter wrote: Because of the schoolgirl and desk's positioning, the reflection should probably be at some sort of angle.

[]:             Quote Post by Yossarian » Sun Jun 06, 2010 8:44 pm They are really thorough in polishing those desks.

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Sun Jun 06, 2010 9:14 pm NAveryW wrote: Reflections don't work that way.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 06, 2010 9:19 pm I suppose itll'be hard for some ppl to see how the angle of the reflex in the desks are off, ill make some sketchs later, im a little lazy now.

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Mon Jun 07, 2010 8:43 am NAveryW wrote: Either every bird is looking in a different direction from the one in which he's flying or their beaks are so tiny as to not register in silhouette at all.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Mon Jun 07, 2010 10:11 am EdZ wrote: Gulls look downwards while feeding, e.g: What I'm more interested in is why seabirds still exist at all, with no ocean life to feed on.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 07, 2010 11:57 am EdZ wrote: What I'm more interested in is why seabirds still exist at all, with no ocean life to feed on.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 07, 2010 12:51 pm EdZ wrote: why seabirds still exist at all, with no ocean life to feed on.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jun 07, 2010 1:01 pm Legendary wrote: There's enough trash around to feed birds for quite awhile.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jun 07, 2010 3:45 pm sephirotic wrote: I agree, there is nothing wrong with the reflex of the birds.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Mon Jun 07, 2010 3:54 pm You know, i do not really mind this thread much /though ok, i find it debating over birds-reflection a tad too much/ but why the "weird" in the title? Visual Errors in 2.22 should do it. Or is it to attract moar or something?

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Mon Jun 07, 2010 4:00 pm It's a worthy thread because colorful and shiny pictures. Probably.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jun 08, 2010 12:59 am You guys, the bird silhouettes look vaguely like angels. C'mon.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jun 08, 2010 1:47 am the_seventh_child wrote: why the "weird" in the title?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue Jun 08, 2010 2:44 am KILL IT. KILL IT QUICKLY.

Here are some of the weirder ones that I've found. They shouldn't be attributed to laziness, considering over a thousand shots were changed between 2.0 and 2.22 , but I guess just to oversight. First, when Unit 05 stabs the Third Angel's entry plug, or dummy plug, or whatever kind of plug it is, the plug disappears... but the plug's shadow remains. The plug is visible again in the next shot: Later on, at the aquarium, the fish are always swimming along in the background. Always, that is, except in this one shot (cropped to emphasize fish): Strangely, the only shot in which the fish are completely motionless is the longest held shot at the picnic in which the fish are visible.
